text:
Video Appointments | Madera County
WATCH: WIC Video Appointments- How to Get Started
Learn how to get started with video appointments at Madera County WIC!
VER: Citas por Video de WIC- Cómo Comenzar
¡Aprenda cómo comenzar con las citas por video en WIC del condado de Madera!
Pause
1
2
WIC offers appointments and classes through a video call!
It's easy!  Your online video appointments will be secure. You can speak “face to face” over Zoom with a:
WIC Counselor
WIC Nutritionist
WIC breastfeeding educator or specialist
To use our WIC video service, you need:
A device like a smart phone, tablet, or computer with a camera and microphone
A good internet connection
An internet browser: Chrome, Firefox, or Safari5+
Download the Zoom app
Online Education Lessons
Madera County WIC uses
wichealth.org
for online education.
It’s simple to get started.
Create an account.
Complete a lesson.
Receive credit from your WIC clinic.
Your device can be a smartphone, tablet, or computer. It’s simple and quick to set up and use. First be sure that your device has Chrome, Firefox, or Safari5+.
When creating an account through WICHealth, the “family ID” section is a specific number assigned to each WIC family. You can find your Family ID in your WIC APP, calling us to ask for it, or we can mail it with the instruction how to create a profile, or via Teletask we can text it to you.
After registering, you will see your dashboard. Click “Start lesson.” On the webpage, you will see your recently completed lessons.
Complete 1-2 lessons from any 1 of the 5 categories you choose.
There are different sections to each lesson:
Breastfeeding
Infants
Children Ages 1-5
Healthy Families
New and Expecting Parents
Fill out and submit the survey at the end of the lesson.
Food benefits will then be loaded to your eWIC card by WIC staff during normal clinic hours (Monday – Friday, 8am – 5pm).
If you have questions about your benefits or completing your nutrition education online, call us at the Madera County WIC office at: (559) 675-7623.
